hook: Five planes took off. None came back. And the rescue plane? Gone too.
full_script:
Five planes took off. None came back. And the rescue plane? Gone too.

December 5th, 1945. Flight 19 — five torpedo bombers, fourteen men — lifts off from Fort Lauderdale on a routine training run. Two hours in, both compasses fail. The flight leader can't tell east from west.

The tower tells him to fly toward the setting sun. He ignores it. Turns the formation north — deeper into the open Atlantic. Fuel runs out. All five planes ditch into the ocean.

The Navy sends a massive flying boat to find them. Thirteen more men. That plane explodes mid-flight.

Twenty-seven men. Six aircraft. Not one piece of wreckage ever found. The Navy blamed the flight leader — until his mother fought back and won. The official cause was changed to two words: reasons unknown.

What really happened to Flight 19? Full story on our channel.
cta_line: What really happened to Flight 19? Full story on our channel.
short_description:
Five planes. Twenty-seven men. Zero wreckage.

#Flight19 #BermudaTriangle #DarkHistory
